subject: Databases
title: Cross Joins and Cartesian Products
description: A 60-second lesson on Cross Joins and Cartesian Products.

hook.text: Three sizes and four colors. CROSS JOIN gives you all 12 combinations — every possible pairing.

buildup.text: CROSS JOIN returns every row from table A combined with every row from table B. No ON clause needed.

discovery.text: It's useful for generating combinations: sizes × colors, dates × products, time slots × rooms.

twist.text: 10,000 rows × 10,000 rows = 100 million results. Cross joins explode fast. Use with small tables only.

climax.text: An accidental cross join (forgetting the ON clause) is a common SQL bug. Spot it by checking row counts.

punchline.text: Cross joins: every combo. Powerful but dangerous at scale.

quiz.question: Why are accidental cross joins dangerous?
options: They produce a massive result set that can overwhelm memory and slow the database
They permanently delete data from both tables
They only work on numeric columns
